action->Lithe.name: Lithe
action->Lithe.description: A riftweasel can fit through tight spaces as if it were a Tiny creature. When Squeezing, it moves at its full speed.

action->Tenacious Grip.name: Tenacious Grip
action->Tenacious Grip.description:
Trigger A creature Escapes from the riftweasel's grapple

Effect The riftweasel twists its body to maintain its grasp, and attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple} the target before they can actually escape. It takes a –5 circumstance penalty to this attempt.

action->Burst from the Dead.name: Burst from the Dead
action->Burst from the Dead.description:
Requirements The riftweasel is inside of a carcass

Effect The riftweasel emerges in a gory burst from the carcass, appearing in any space adjacent to the remains. As it bursts out, it shrieks and splatters decay all about it; the riftweasel attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} an observed creature. The riftweasel doesn't take any penalty to this check for not speaking during the attempt, and if it critically succeeds, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by fragments of gore and putrescence splattered onto it by the unsettling display.

action->Carrion Eater.name: Carrion Eater
action->Carrion Eater.description: The riftweasel consumes flesh from an adjacent dead creature and regains @Damage[10d6[healing]] Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

action->Hide in Carcass.name: Hide in Carcass
action->Hide in Carcass.description: The riftweasel Burrows into the carcass of a Large or larger adjacent creature, then @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Hides}. As long as it remains inside a carcass, it can sense the surrounding area via tremorsense, and gains greater cover. A riftweasel doesn't need to hold its breath while burrowed into a carcass.

action->Snap Neck.name: Snap Neck
action->Snap Neck.description:
The riftweasel wrenches at the head of a Medium or smaller creature it has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}; the creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:41] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[2d10[bludgeoning]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d10[bludgeoning]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d10[bludgeoning]] damage and is permanently paralyzed.

Root Rotter.name: Root Rotter
Root Rotter.publicNotes:
Root rotters are all that remain of the unfortunate elven residents of Greengold. Some died in the initial bursts of Jeharlu spores, while others succumbed to infestation by sinmolds. Fungus now riddles their bodies, wrapping around their brains and nerves to reanimate them.

Corruption Spreaders

A root rotter that remains in place for 24 hours causes a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] to become overgrown with foul-smelling unpleasant fungus. This spread increases by 5 feet for each additional 24 hours the root rotter remains motionless, to a maximum area of a @Template[type:emanation|distance:60] after 12 days of motionless lurking. This fungus is permanent as long as it has a source of nourishment but can be destroyed as easily as any patch of mundane fungus.

action->Fungus Sense.name: Fungus Sense
action->Fungus Sense.description: A root rotter has imprecise tremorsense to detect the vibrations of creatures who are within a region of fungal terrain shared by the root rotter.

action->Fungal Empowerment.name: Fungal Empowerment
action->Fungal Empowerment.description: If the root rotter is touching fungal terrain at the start of its turn, they regain @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} that turn; they can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

action->Fungal Mind.name: Fungal Mind
action->Fungal Mind.description: Root rotters communicate telepathically with each other. While within telepathic range of at least one other root rotter, they gain a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls, Perception checks, and saving throws against mental effects. If one root rotter is aware of a combatant, all members of the fungal mind within range are aware of it.

action->Disperse.name: Disperse
action->Disperse.description:
Requirements The root rotter has 25 Hit Points or fewer

Effect The root rotter destroys themself. Thorny fungal roots explode from their body, dealing @Damage[10d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save). Any creature who fails the save is exposed to rotter infestation.

action->Fungal Root.name: Fungal Root
action->Fungal Root.description:
Requirements The root rotter is standing in fungal terrain

Effect Fungal roots reach out to snag a nearby foe. An enemy within 30 feet of the root rotter takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]] damage and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:27] saving throw.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the end of its next turn.

Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes clumsy 1 with an unlimited duration. In addition, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} ([[/act escape dc=27]]) for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure but double damage. In addition, the creature is exposed to rotter infestation.

action->Rotter Infestation.name: Rotter Infestation
action->Rotter Infestation.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:27]

Onset 1 day

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and can sense the presence of root rotters within 30 feet as an imprecise sense (1 round)

Stage 3 as stage 2, but transformation into a root rotter upon death (1 day)

action->Otherworldly Will.name: Otherworldly Will
action->Otherworldly Will.description: The rotting cultist is driven forward by a singular, otherworldly will, guiding their actions. Whenever the rotting cultist attempts a Will saving throw against a mental effect that would control or dictate its actions, the rotting cultist treats the result of the saving throw as one category better.

action->Fungal Fervor.name: Fungal Fervor
action->Fungal Fervor.description:
Trigger The rotting cultist is reduced to 0 HP

Effect The rotting cultist avoids death and reduces their HP to 1. They are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}, and each time they use this ability, their slowed value increases by 1, up to a maximum of 3. Each time they use this ability, a bloom of fungus erupts from their flesh as their movements become even more disjointed. This slowed condition is reduced by 1 each day. Note that since this is a reaction, any further damage done to a rotting cultist that reduces them to 0 HP before they regain their reactions kills them.

action->Pray to the Rotting God.name: Den Verrottenden Gott anbeten
action->Pray to the Rotting God.description:
Häufigkeit Einmal am Tag

Effekt Der Verrottende Kultist ruft den Verrottenden Gott an und bietet ihm sein Fleisch im Austausch für Macht an. Der Verrottende Kultist wird um @Damage[(4d6+8)[healing]]{4W6+8 TP} geheilt, erhält 1 Minute lang einen Zustandsbonus von +1 auf Angriffswürfe und reduziert alle ihn betreffenden Zustände (außer Verlangsamt) um 1 (sofern sie einen numerischen Wert haben; dies hat keine Auswirkung auf Zustände ohne Werte). Der Verrottende Kultist erhält den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Verlangsamt 1} für 10 Minuten, während eine Pilzblüte aus seinem Fleisch hervorbricht.

action->Sneak Attack.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.AWvNPE4U0kEJSL1T]

The rotting cultist deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Surprise Attack.name: Surprise Attack
action->Surprise Attack.description: On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the rotting cultist.

Second Spawn.name: Second Spawn
Second Spawn.publicNotes:
The timber body of a second spawn cracks with each movement, as if its very frame protests its abominable mobility. Grown from arboreals native to the Plane of Wood, the greatest of the second spawn—in effect, the "First Spawn"—is Treerazer's mobile fortress, the Witchbole.

Cleftwood fiends blend the properties of demons and wood elementals, their timber bodies distorted by burls, rot, and fungal growths. Planar scholars have debated into which category cleftwood fiends are better categorized, but to cleftwoods themselves there is no question—they feel much more at home in the Outer Rifts than they do the Plane of Wood.

Demonic Elementals

Cleftwood fiends aren't true demons or elementals, but they possess both of those creature traits. Since they aren't formed from sinful souls, they lack the typical sin vulnerability most demons possess, and since they possess blood-like sap, they lack the typical immunity to bleed most elementals possess.

Other Cleftwood Fiends

Rift pulpers and second spawn are the only types of cleftwood fiend currently active in Tanglebriar, but others exist in the Outer Rifts, particularly in the realm of Jeharlu. Here, one might encounter the blood-drinking stump-like splintertooth, the humanoid primal-magic-wielding blighted gardener, or the enormous horror of the fell deadfall.

action->Spray Sap.name: Spray Sap
action->Spray Sap.description:
Trigger The second spawn takes damage

Effect A spray of bloody sap spurts from the second spawn's wound, targeting a creature within 30 feet of the second spawn's choice. That creature takes @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:41|basic] save) and is exposed to corrupting sap.

action->Corrupting Sap.name: Corrupting Sap
action->Corrupting Sap.description:
The first time in a round a creature is exposed to the second spawn's corrupting sap, the creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected, and is temporarily immune to corrupting sap for 1 minute.

Success Intrusive fiendish thoughts and cruel urges well up in the creature's mind, causing the creature to become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the end of its next turn.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is controlled by the second spawn for 1 minute.

Sewer Pipe Trap.name: Sewer Pipe Trap
Sewer Pipe Trap.description: Pressure-sensitive panels in the floor connects to a metal plate holding a number of sewer pipes shut in the ceiling.
Sewer Pipe Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:32] (expert) on the floor panels before the plate falls away

action->Filth Fall.name: Filth Fall
action->Filth Fall.description:
Trigger Pressure is placed on the floor tiles

Effect The metal plate above gives way, unleashing a torrent of sewage and flesh-eating worms on everyone within 10 feet of the pressure plate. Anyone hit by the filth must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} and takes @Damage[2d12[piercing]] damage.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} and takes @Damage[4d12[piercing]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 4} and takes @Damage[4d12[piercing]] damage plus @Damage[2d12[persistent,piercing]] damage.

action->Resilient Hunter.name: Resilient Hunter
action->Resilient Hunter.description: When Shalelu rolls a success on a Fortitude or Reflex save, she gets a critical success instead.

action->Favored Terrain.name: Favored Terrain
action->Favored Terrain.description: Shalelu gains a Climb speed of 35 feet when she's in forested terrain.

action->Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
action->Hunt Prey.description: Shalelu designates a single creature she can see and hear, or one she's Tracking, as her prey. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} the prey and to Survival checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]{Track} the prey. The first time she hits her designated prey in a round, she deals an additional 2d8 precision damage. These effects last until she uses Hunt Prey again.

action->Nature's Edge.name: Nature's Edge
action->Nature's Edge.description: Enemies are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to Shalelu when they're in difficult terrain.

action->Unimpeded Journey.name: Unimpeded Journey
action->Unimpeded Journey.description: Shalelu ignores the effects of difficult terrain and treats greater difficult terrain as difficult terrain.

Shroud Moss.name: Shroud Moss
Shroud Moss.publicNotes:
Shroud mosses are often confused for ghosts, as they appear as a shimmering, insubstantial imprint of a deceased creature. In truth, nothing of the deceased remains within a shroud moss. Rather, what appears to be a ghost is an image created by clouds of shimmering spores surrounding the wispy tendrils of the moss itself. When not disguised by a stolen shape, a shroud moss appears as a mass of gray-green tendrils of moss capable of shifting its shape into the basic framework of a creature, looking not unlike a free-standing humanoid nervous system with a mass of tangles where a brain would be.

Grayshroud Sufferers

Those who become infected with grayshroud may at first assume they're just suffering from overexertion or lack of sleep, but once they become drained by the disease, their flesh turns increasingly gray and is riddled with flaky sheets as their skin takes on the appearance of peeling bark. Those who die from grayshroud move on to the afterlife as normal, but their remains spawn a new shroud moss who adds their form to its stolen shapes.

action->Spore Telepathy.name: Spore Telepathy
action->Spore Telepathy.description: A shroud moss can communicate via telepathy with any creature within its sporecloud aura, doing so in that creature's native language.

action->Filament Body.name: Filament Body
action->Filament Body.description: A shroud moss's body is physical, but much lighter and flexible than normal. They can move through openings down to 1 inch in diameter with ease and are treated as Tiny creatures for all effects affected by a creature's size. A shroud moss adjusts its saving throws against wind-based effects down by one degree of success.

action->Frightful Presence.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.etMnv73EIdEZrYYu]

Requirements Assume Shape is active.

60 feet. @Check[will|dc:37|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

action->Sporecloud.name: Sporecloud
action->Sporecloud.description: 60 feet. The shroud moss constantly exudes a transparent cloud of spores; creatures in the aura can communicate with the shroud moss via telepathy. A creature that ends its turn in the aura is exposed to grayshroud. The sporecloud's range is reduced to 5 feet until the end of the shroud moss's next turn whenever the shroud moss is exposed to winds stronger than a gentle breeze.

action->Assume Shape.name: Assume Shape
action->Assume Shape.description: The shroud moss assumes the ghostly (and often unsettlingly distorted) shape of the creature from whose physical remains it grew from, or from a creature it damaged this round, for 1 minute. While in this form, the shroud moss's frightful presence aura is active, and its tendril attacks inflict an additional 3d6 mental damage as feelings of betrayal and imminent death wrack the target's mind. This increases to 5d6 mental damage if the shroud moss attacks the creature it stole this shape from.

action->Grayshroud.name: Grayshroud
action->Grayshroud.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:40]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and fatigued (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and fatigued (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3}, fatigued, and can't speak (1 day)

Stage 5 death

action->Desperation Vulnerability.name: Desperation Vulnerability
action->Desperation Vulnerability.description: Sinmolds are aware that they're in a vulnerable state as formless "newborn fiends" and are desperate to become stronger before they become prey to more powerful demons. Whenever the sinmold is in a combat encounter and ends its turn without dealing damage to another creature, the sinmold takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

action->Digestion.name: Digestion
action->Digestion.description: The first time in a round when the sinmold damages a creature with Engulf, the sinmold immediately restores Hit Points equal to the damage dealt, gaining Hit Points in excess of its maximum as temporary Hit Points that last for 1 hour. As long as the sinmold possesses these temporary Hit Points, its surface hardens, and its AC increases to 30.

action->Engulf.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.zU3Ovaet4xQ5Gmvy]

@Check[reflex|dc:32], @Damage[3d10[acid]] damage plus digestion, [[/act escape dc=32]], Rupture 18

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Engulf]

action->Sinmold Infestation.name: Sinmold Infestation
action->Sinmold Infestation.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:32]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d8[poison]] damage and can hear the thoughts of root rotters within 30 feet as an imprecise sense (1 round)

Stage 3 as stage 2, but @Damage[4d8[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (1 round)

Stage 4 as stage 3, and arise as a root rotter upon death (1 round)

action->Spore Purge.name: Spore Purge
action->Spore Purge.description:
Requirements The sinmold has temporary Hit Points from digestion

Effect The sinmold loses all temporary Hit Points and releases any creatures it currently has engulfed, pushing them into the nearest unoccupied spaces. It releases a cloud of spores from its body, dealing @Damage[5d8[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save) in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20]. Any creature that fails this save also must attempt a save against sinmold infestation.

Soul Pod.name: Soul Pod
Soul Pod.description: An oblong, puffball-like fungus shaped ominously like a bulging coffin in which Aravashnial's distorted face can be seen silently screaming.
Soul Pod.disable: @Check[religion|dc:40] three times to exorcise Aravashnial's soul from a pod, or @Check[nature|dc:43] twice to pull the well-hidden filaments that connect the spore pod to the rest of the spires
Soul Pod.reset: A soul pod deactivates once no PCs are in the spires, then resets automatically.
Soul Pod.routine:
(2 actions) The soul pod releases a soulrending shriek on its first action, then on its second action exhales a blast of spore-laden wind in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40]. Creatures in this area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. This blast has the air and poison traits.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} by the wind.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[16d6[poison]] damage, is knocked prone, and is blown 10 feet backward directly away from the soul pod, possibly being blown off the edge of a platform or stairwell.

action->Soulrending Shriek.name: Soulrending Shriek
action->Soulrending Shriek.description:
Trigger A round begins after all demons in the spires have been slain

Effect Aravashnial's distorted face unleashes an overwhelming shriek of agony that threatens to completely warp the thoughts of all intruders. All creatures within 20 feet of a soul pod must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure). A confused creature can attempt a new save at the end of each of their turns to end the confusion early. The soul pods then roll initiative.

Spawn of Jeharlu.name: Brut von Jeharlu
Spawn of Jeharlu.publicNotes:
Die Bruten von Jeharlu schweben normalerweise in den Lüften über dem Reich Jeharlu und genießen blasphemische und schreckliche Träume und Fantasien, während sie die Pilzwelt unter sich nach Eindringlingen absuchen. Es ist nicht weit hergeholt, eine Brut von Jeharlu mit einem weißen Blutkörperchen zu vergleichen, nur dass es sich in diesem Fall bei dem Körper, den sie verteidigen, um Jeharlu handelt. Außerhalb dieses Reiches kehrt sich die Rolle der Bruten um - nun sind sie die Infektionserreger, die überall, wo sie hinkommen, versuchen, Verderbnis über den Boden, die Vegetation und alles Lebendige zu verbreiten.

Jeharlu-Sporen

Baumschnitter setzt Jeharlu-Sporen als Eröffnungszug gegen Kyonin ein, um plötzlich und explosiv Fungusplagen zu verbreiten. Genau diese Sporen entwickeln sich zu Bruten von Jeharlu, wenn sie in den Äußeren Klüften zum Keimen ausgesetzt werden.

action->Scent Purity.name: Reinheitsgespür
action->Scent Purity.description: Eine Brut von Jeharlu kann heilige Kreaturen mit ihrem Geruchssinn bis auf 120 Fuß/36 m genau aufspüren.

action->Grasping Strands.name: Greifende Stränge
action->Grasping Strands.description:
Auslöser Eine Kreatur innerhalb von 60 Fuß/18 m um die Brut von Jeharlu nutzt eine Bewegungsaktion oder verlässt während einer Bewegungsaktion, die sie ausführt, ein Feld

Effekt Eine Reihe von Ranken peitscht hervor, sodass die Brut von Jeharlu gegen die auslösende Kreatur einen Fertigkeitswurf für Athletik zum @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Ergreifen} ablegen kann. Die Brut kann die Kreatur heran Ziehen, wenn diese im nächsten Zug der Brut noch Gegriffen ist.

action->Hallucinatory Spores.name: Halluzinogene Sporen
action->Hallucinatory Spores.description: 60 Fuß/18 m. Die Brut ist von Sporen umgeben, die die Gedanken derjenigen beeinflussen, die ihnen ausgesetzt sind. Wenn eine Kreatur ihren Zug in der Aura beendet und nicht den Atem anhält, muss sie einen @Check[will|dc:42]{Willenswurf} ablegen. Bei einem Fehlschlag, erhält sie bis zum Ende ihres nächsten Zuges den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Verwirrt} (bei einem Kritischen Fehlschlag für 1 Minute).

action->Drain Soul.name: Seelenentzug
action->Drain Soul.description:
Die Brut streckt Fressstiele aus, um einer angrenzenden @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Gebunden} oder hilflosen Kreatur die Seele und das Leben zu entziehen. Das Ziel erleidet @Damage[10d6[spirit]]{10W6 Seelenschaden} und muss einen @Check[will|dc:45]{Willenswurf} ablegen. Wenn der Schaden durch Seelenentzug die Trefferpunkte einer Kreatur auf 0 reduziert, stirbt diese Kreatur sofort.

Kritischer Erfolg Das Ziel erleidet keinen Schaden.

Erfolg Das Ziel erleidet halben Schaden und erhält den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Ausgelaugt 1}.

Fehlschlag Das Ziel erleidet vollen Schaden und erhält den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Ausgelaugt 2}. Hatte es bereits den Zustand Ausgelaugt, erhöht sich sein Zustandswert um 1 bis maximal @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Ausgelaugt 4}.

Kritischer Fehlschlag Wie Fehlschlag, aber mit doppeltem Schaden und das Ziel erhält den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Todgeweiht 1}.

Spear Jaw Trap.name: Spear Jaw Trap
Spear Jaw Trap.description: Motion-sensitive fungus tendrils cause spears to shoot out of the floor and ceiling, almost like teeth.
Spear Jaw Trap.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:30] (expert) on the spear panels from any adjacent space, or @Check[nature|dc:32] (master) to slowly scrape away the motion-sensitive tendrils without triggering the trap
Spear Jaw Trap.reset: The trap resets in 1 round after a creature leaves its space.

action->Spear Jaws.name: Spear Jaws
action->Spear Jaws.description:
Trigger A Small or larger creature enters the square marked with an S

Effect Dozens of iron spears shoot from the floor and ceiling, impaling anyone in the space. The trap deals @Damage[(8d8+16)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). Those that fail this save are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until the start of their next turn when the spears retract.

Spore Queen Quilindra.name: Spore Queen Quilindra
Spore Queen Quilindra.publicNotes:
Hundreds of years ago, a cadre of powerful succubus assassins invaded the Abyssal realm of the demon lord of disease and fungus, Cyth-V'sug. These succubi sought to slay a powerful witch who had befouled a profane temple the assassins served. The succubi failed at their mission, but from their corpses grew the first fungus tyrants. Pleased with this development, the witch then seeded numerous worlds on the Material Plane with fungus tyrant spores, who have since grown and thrived in their new environments as rot thrives in a corpse. Today, hundreds of fungus tyrants inhabit the fouler locales of the Material Plane. They're particularly fond of large cave systems and ruined cities, but also can be found in corrupted woodlands where blighted fey rule or in the sewers of active cities that spew vast amounts of magical waste.

An encounter with a normally unintelligent plant monster acting with methodical intent can be evidence of a fungus tyrant's manipulations, but fungus tyrants are equally fond of keeping other types of creatures as pets and thralls—particularly attractive humanoids. Especially favored minions are given the gift of the fungus tyrant's embrace and are transformed into fungoid minions forever loyal to their pallid ruler. Yet most fungus tyrants realize that a diverse array of guardians is best, and they often choose to keep some non-fungus minions on hand to protect their lair.

Fungus tyrants are violently jealous. Any who attempt to lure away their dominated pets or cure those who have been infected by them earns their eternal ire. Yet it's succubi who enrage fungus tyrants the most. In these demons, the fungus tyrant sees a former glamour and power they no longer have access to, and only the brutal destruction of these mocking memories can salve a fungus tyrant's anger. A fungus tyrant faced with someone whom they suspect might be a succubus is terrible indeed, for in such battles, the fiendish fungal beings abandon their normal approach of subtle mental trickery and bring all of their power to bear in an attempt to rip the offending creature apart. Fungus tyrants also take great delight in transforming succubi into fungoid creatures under their control.

While their jealousy doesn't compel them to do battle with other fungus tyrants, they're always careful to maintain their own territories apart from any nearby kin, and they take great pains not to "poach" other tyrant's charmed and infested pets.

A fungus tyrant is 6 feet tall and weighs 150 pounds.

Spore Thralls

When a creature becomes a spore thrall, it rises from death with the same statistics it had in life, but gains the fungus trait, as well as immunity to death effects, disease, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, and poison, and its alignment switches to match that of the fungus tyrant who created them. When a spore thrall is slain, its soul finally travels to the Boneyard for judgment, but the fungus-encrusted physical remains often spawn new fungal creatures, such as myceloids, violet fungi, or drakauthixes. These fungal spawn are not controlled by the fungus tyrant but generally regard them as an ally or, more often, a divinity.

action->Fungaltongue.name: Fungaltongue
action->Fungaltongue.description: The Spore Queen can speak with all forms of fungi. She gains a +3 circumstance bonus to all Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation checks made against fungal creatures.

action->Distracted by Failures.name: Distracted by Failures
action->Distracted by Failures.description:
Quilindra remembers her failures in her previous life vividly. A PC can taunt her as an action with the auditory, concentrate, linguistic, and mental traits by attempting a @Check[deception|dc:40], @Check[diplomacy|dc:40], @Check[intimidation|dc:40], or @Check[performance|dc:40] check.

Critical Success Quilindra is significantly distracted; she becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for the remainder of the encounter, becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of her next turn as she spends valuable moments sputtering and shrieking in rage at the PC. Additional critical successes increase the value of her stupefied condition by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3}.

Success As critical success, but she's not stupefied and remains off-guard only until the end of her next turn.

Failure Quilindra is only slightly distracted and becomes off-guard to that PC until the end of the PC's turn.

Critical Failure Quilindra is unaffected.

action->Create Spawn.name: Create Spawn
action->Create Spawn.description:
The Spore Queen can create spawn from foes, but she doesn't use this ability in combat, as she would rather see the PCs dead than given this fungal gift.

If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by a fungus tyrant's Mind-Draining Kiss, the fungus tyrant can turn this victim into a spore thrall by donating some of their own fungal matter to the victim and leaving them to "ripen" within a patch of fungus for 24 hours. After this time, the creature is restored to life but gains the fungus trait and becomes a spore thrall. This prevents the creature's soul from continuing on to the afterlife in a similar manner to how undead are taken out of that cycle—as such, those who hold that cycle sacred (particularly worshipers of Pharasma) regard spore thralls as bad as the undead, despite them being living creatures. If the new spore thrall is a lower level than its creator, then it's under the creator's control. If a fungus tyrant controls too many spore thralls at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spore thralls can free themselves by succeeding at a @Check[will|dc:28] save. Typically, a fungus tyrant avoids creating spore thralls out of fallen foes who are too powerful to control, for they're only too aware of the compulsion for revenge such victims might harbor for those who wrenched them from the afterlife.

action->Grow Sporepod.name: Grow Sporepod
action->Grow Sporepod.description: The Spore Queen causes a human-sized pod of fungal material to burst out of the ground at any point she can see within 60 feet. Once created, a sporepod lasts for 1 minute, until she moves more than 120 feet away from it, or until it's destroyed (AC 45, Hardness 20, 60 HP). She cannot Grow a Sporepod again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Grow Sporepod]]{1d4 rounds}, but apart from this and a sporepod's 1 minute life, there is no hard limit to the number of sporepods she can have active at any one time.

action->Mind-Draining Kiss.name: Mind-Draining Kiss
action->Mind-Draining Kiss.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The Spore Queen engages a creature she's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} in an act of passion in an attempt to drain its will. The kiss makes the creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} or increases its stupefied condition by 1, to a maximum of 4. The creature takes @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and must then succeed at a @Check[will|dc:42] save or be affected by a suggestion to remain immobile rather than trying to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape}.

action->Sporestride.name: Sporestride
action->Sporestride.description: The Spore Queen Steps into an adjacent sporepod or non-destroyed soul pod (see area K) and instantly teleports to any other active sporepod or soul pod in the area, bursting out of the target pod and destroying it in the process.

action->Sporewarp.name: Sporewarp
action->Sporewarp.description: The Spore Queen attempts a tentacle Strike but treats a sporepod or soul pod of her choice within 120 feet that she can see as the point of origin of that tentacle Strike.

action->Vengeful Slice.name: Vengeful Slice
action->Vengeful Slice.description: The Spore Queen inflicts an additional 2d6 spirit damage to elves she Strikes with her dagger. An elf reduced to 0 Hit Points by the Spore Queen's dagger Strike becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} as Treerazer takes note of their soul.

Spore Stomach.description: A puffball-like fungus periodically vents gray spores into the air.
Spore Stomach.disable: @Check[nature|dc:37] (master) three times to carefully prune away the fungal roots that connect to the garden below, or @Check[religion|dc:37] (master) three times to exorcise the demonic energies from the fungus
Spore Stomach.reset: The spore stomach deactivates if it starts a turn and there are no non-fungus creatures in area E1. It then immediately resets.
Spore Stomach.routine: (1 action; divine, poison) On the first round, the spore stomach sprays a 40-foot-long cone of spores into the left half of area E1. On the second round, it sprays spores into the right half of area E1. On the third round, it takes no action. It repeats this pattern thereafter every 3 rounds. A creature in the area of spores takes @Damage[10d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic|traits:divine,poison] save).

action->Spore Burst.description: When a spore thrall is reduced to 0 Hit Points by damage other than cold damage, they burst in an explosion of toxic green spores. action->Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
action->Hunt Prey.description: The spore thrall designates a single creature they can see or hear as their prey. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when they @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} their prey and ignore the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment against the prey they're hunting. If the spore thrall uses Hunt Prey against a creature when they already have one designated, the previous creature loses the designation. The designation lasts for 24 hours.

action->Precise Aim.name: Precise Aim
action->Precise Aim.description: A spore thrall deals an additional 2d6 precision damage against a creature they've designated as their hunted prey.

Spore Tyrannosaurus.name: Spore Tyrannosaurus
Spore Tyrannosaurus.publicNotes:
Widely regarded as the king of the dinosaurs, the tyrannosaurus is a massive predator with a wide mouth filled with viciously sharp teeth. Some tribes of giants have even trained tyrannosauruses as mounts or beasts of war.

Remnants from the world's primeval era, these enormous reptilian animals still exist in large numbers in remote wildernesses or underground in magical Darklands caverns. Lizardfolk, orcs, giants, and other humanoids who live near dinosaurs use the animals as mounts, guards, or hunting beasts. Occasionally, rich nobles will collect dinosaurs to display them in menageries, which almost inevitably leads to cast-offs being nursed back to health by druids and other champions of nature. When dinosaurs establish themselves in regions outside their normal habitats, it's often the result of a large collection being released.

action->Fungalsense.name: Fungalsense

action->Aura of Spores.name: Aura of Spores
action->Aura of Spores.description: 30 feet. Creatures that start their turn in the spore tyrannosaurus's aura are in danger of inhaling the toxic spores that it constantly exudes. Creatures without the fungus or plant trait take @Damage[4d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save) upon entering the aura or starting their turn in the aura. Those who fail are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the spores.

action->Fungal Empowerment.name: Fungal Empowerment
action->Fungal Empowerment.description: While the spore tyrannosaurus is touching fungal corruption, it gains fast healing 10 and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened}; it can use the extra action to Stride or Strike.

The creature falls 25 feet (assuming the tyrannosaurus flings it as high as it can) and takes falling damage accordingly. If the flung creature lands on another creature, the creature it lands on takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage. The creature being landed on can attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save.

A tyrannosaurus gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack a creature it has pinned in this manner but can only Swallow Whole if that creature is grabbed with its jaws.

Those who fail are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The fungal tyrannosaurus can't use the spore breath weapon again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge the spore breath weapon]]{1d4 rounds}.

Swamp Blight.name: Swamp Blight
Swamp Blight.publicNotes:
A swamp blight appears as a quivering blob of rancid brown and green mud from which dozens of hateful red eyes peer.

Blights are corruptions of nature's wrath.

Swampy Undead

While a swamp blight can dominate undead in its cursed domain, it prefers to use this power to gather and control undead that would prosper in a swampy environment. When building an encounter with a swamp blight, consider adjusting existing undead to match more thematically with the swampland environment, and avoid using undead that wouldn't make sense in such a region. Adjusting an undead creature's innate spells to be primal spells associated with swampy regions is a great way to achieve this customization.

When a blight targets a mindless fungi, ooze, or plant, its dominate spell loses the mental trait.

action->Call of the Mire.name: Call of the Mire
action->Call of the Mire.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect An aura of glittering lights surrounds the swamp blight, compelling a target the swamp blight can see that's within 60 feet to move toward it. This is not forced movement and can compel creatures to move through dangerous terrain. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:38] save.

Critical Success The creature is temporarily immune to Call of the Mire for 1 hour.

Success The target immediately Strides or otherwise uses their fastest Speed to approach the swamp blight. This movement does not cost the target their actions. On the target's next turn, they cannot willingly move farther away from the blight, but they can otherwise act normally.

Failure As success, except that the target spends all of their actions on their next turn also approaching the blight if they are not already adjacent, and they cannot take any other actions for 1 round. Additionally, the target cannot willingly move farther away from the blight for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, except that the creature's only actions are to approach the blight for 1 minute.

action->Cursed Domain.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.h6rf7Z7Vy1OD1YFw]

Humanoid creatures that die in a swamp blight's cursed domain are infused with void energy. After 24 hours, they rise as undead creatures of their level of lower. Creatures whose bodies were intact typically become zombies or mummies, while those whose bodies were severely damaged typically rise as shadows or bhutas. These undead are not automatically under the swamp blight's direct control, but they can potentially be dominated by the swamp blight.

Once per year, when in a terrain that is compatible with its type (such as a swamp for a swamp blight), the blight can infuse the land around it with its corrupted essence by performing a rite that takes one day. The region in a 5-mile radius becomes its cursed domain; this effect does not extend into incompatible terrain and does not move with the blight. The point at which the blight cursed the domain becomes its epicenter.

Within their cursed domain, a blight ignores all non-magical difficult terrain and always gains the benefits of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SB7cMECVtE06kByk]{Cover Tracks} action. Blights also have an imprecise sense for the locations of all creatures within their domain and can communicate with all sapient creatures they detect within their domain using telepathy. Each blight's cursed domain has additional properties specific to the blight's type. A blight outside of a cursed domain becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until it returns to its own or another blight's cursed domain.

Removing this curse requires a character with the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.zGrUu3dUtqHrqGuo]{Break Curse} feat (or a similar ability); this activity must be performed at the cursed domain's epicenter. If the blight that cursed the domain is dead, checks to counteract a cursed domain gain a +4 circumstance bonus.

Tanglebriar Regent.name: Tanglebriar Regent
Tanglebriar Regent.publicNotes:
Arboreal regents are lumbering, solitary creatures responsible for guarding an entire forest. They take an especially long view of affairs and never act brashly or without much deliberation. They occasionally come together in small groups called groves to share news and pass their wisdom down to the arboreal wardens that have sprouted under their watch. In times of grave danger, all the groves in a region may gather for a great months-long meeting to plan and, eventually, act upon a threat.

The typical arboreal regent is 30 feet tall, has a trunk 2 feet in diameter, and weighs 4,500 pounds.

Arboreals are guardians of the forest and representatives of the trees. As long-lived as the woods they watch over, arboreals consider themselves parents and shepherds of trees rather than their gardeners. Consequently, while arboreals tend to be slow and methodical, they are terrifyingly swift when forced to fight in defense of the woods. Though they rarely seek out the companionship of short-lived folk—even elves are fugacious in the eyes of arboreals—and have an inherent distrust of change, arboreals have been known to tolerate those who seek to learn from their long-winded, rambling monologues, especially if such pupils also express a desire to protect the timberlands. Against those who threaten their realm, such as loggers eager to harvest lumber or settlers aiming to establish croplands or a town, arboreals' wrath is unwavering and devastating. Perhaps ironically, arboreals are gifted at tearing down what others build—a trait that serves vengeful members of their kind well.

The catoblepas is an aggressive beast at the best of times. Though it prefers swamps, the catoblepas has been know to forage in plains and forests for short periods, leaving behind hunting grounds tainted by its foul breath and noxious waste that other predators and prey alike avoid for days or even weeks thereafter. The catoblepas bullies those creatures it believes are a match for it, and eats everything weaker.

A catoblepas is 15 feet long and weighs 2,200 pounds.

Tithekeeper.name: Tithekeeper
Tithekeeper.publicNotes:
Razmir's most powerful priests deploy tithekeepers to ensure his followers don't develop sticky fingers. These ziggurat-like constructs have over two dozen legs and a trap-like mouth. At its top, a mask with a colored ribbon attached denotes its symbolic rank in the Church of Razmir.

Instruments of Terror

Though the tithekeeper's primary function is to collect coins, they're capable of great violence. Priests who determine a village is too far behind on payments might have their tithekeeper skewer multiple residents and wave its victims' bodies in the air as it parades through the streets. Those who survive the ordeal are highly motivated to avoid a repeat experience.

action->Eyes Behind the Mask.name: Eyes Behind the Mask
action->Eyes Behind the Mask.description: A creature wearing the mask and garb of a priest of Razmir that outranks the tithekeeper's mask and ribbon rank can spend an hour Interacting with the tithekeeper's mask to connect with it. The creature's mask becomes an invested item that allows them to see and hear as though peering through the tithekeeper's mask. The creature must use a 3-action activity envisioning the tithekeeper to activate the scrying effect, and the effect can be Dismissed. Multiple creatures can create connections to the tithekeeper's mask simultaneously, and a creature can have connections to multiple tithekeepers with just one mask. Each connection ends after a week but can be renewed at any time.

action->Accept Tithe.name: Accept Tithe
action->Accept Tithe.description:
Trigger A creature drops or offers at least 750 gp in valuables within 15 feet of the tithekeeper

Effect The tithekeeper quickly uses its feet to scoop the valuables into its vault and is now indifferent toward the triggering creature unless that creature uses hostile actions against it or its allies.

action->Capacity for Punishment.name: Capacity for Punishment
action->Capacity for Punishment.description: The tithekeeper can have up to six creatures impaled and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} with its feet at once.

action->Impale.name: Impale

Ulugurnix.name: Ulugurnix
Ulugurnix.publicNotes:
The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon. Notably among these are the massive paired horns of the horned dragon. While their bulky frames, natural coloration, and prominent ridged scales are all remarkable in their own way, it's the horns that are most obvious and striking at first glance. Horned dragons use their horns to impale their prey in a quick and brutal display of their might. They are generally contemplative and have a fixation on knowledge and self-discipline, traits belied by their bestial appearance. As a result, horned dragons are generally more open to speaking with outsiders.

Dragons come in myriad forms, with many having magical abilities or connections to magic. Some dragons draw greater power from magic than others, allowing them to manifest abilities or alter their physiques with prolonged exposure to magic. These dragons become more powerful as they age and strengthen their connections with their magical origins. Scholars debate the classification of these dragons, with some preferring the name magical dragons and others using traditional dragons due to their connection to specific magical traditions. Regardless of their names, these dragons share a number of characteristics: their ability to tap into magical energies, intensified might and cunning as they grow older, and an inclination to hoard vast amounts of treasure and wealth.

Unrisen Slithermaw.name: Unrisen Slithermaw
Unrisen Slithermaw.publicNotes:
An unrisen is a mangled conglomeration of splintered bones, decaying organs, and rotting flesh, created when a ritual such as resurrect goes catastrophically wrong. Immense care must be taken, for if the ritual is a critical failure, an unrisen can be the result—as many a ritualist has learned to their horror.

Unrisen are barely intelligent, aware only of the agony constantly inflicted by their flawed creation and their resentment for the living. They tend to attack the casters involved in the botched ritual first before lashing out at everyone else around them. Though an unrisen's twisted form is unrecognizable as the intended target of the resurrection, its wordless howls are often disturbingly similar to the deceased's voice. If an unrisen is destroyed before it can rise again, it's reduced to a handful of metallic blue-green salts referred to as essential salts.

Vansidieth.name: Vansidieth
Vansidieth.publicNotes:
Vansidieths rise from the souls of politicians or generals whose pride resulted in great social suffering and tragedy.

Demonic Generals

While some consider demons to be bickering monsters known more for infighting than uniting in a single army, the story of the Worldwound teaches an important lesson. A powerful demon can organize demonic hordes into an incredibly destructive war machine, and vansidieths often lead those armies as generals, coaxing cooperation from the fiendish rabble.

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

Craft Requirements Supply a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.

backpack->Retrieval Belt (Major).name: Retrieval Belt (Major)
backpack->Retrieval Belt (Major).description:
This belt is covered in small pouches that clasp with buttons of painstakingly carved stone. The belt is tied to an extradimensional space that can hold up to ten items of 1 Bulk or less. Anyone holding the belt can sense its contents, but only those who've invested it can store or retrieve items. Many retrieval belts are found with an item already inside.

Activate—Store Item 1 (manipulate)

Requirements There is room for an item in the belt

Effect One item you're holding with a Bulk of 1 or less vanishes into the belt's extradimensional space.

Activate—Retrieve Item F (manipulate)

Requirements An item is stored in the belt and you have a free hand

Effect The item stored in the belt appears in your hand. Neither Store Item nor Retrieve Item can be activated again for 1 minute.

action->Otherworldly Will.name: Otherworldly Will
action->Otherworldly Will.description: Vintalax is driven forward by a singular, otherworldly will, guiding their actions. Whenever Vintalax attempts a Will saving throw against a mental effect that would control or dictate their actions, Vintalax treats the result of the saving throw as one category better.

action->Spore Burst.name: Spore Burst
action->Spore Burst.description:
Trigger Vintalax takes damage from a melee Strike made by an adjacent creature

Effect Vintalax's corrupted body unleashes a cloud of spores that envelopes the attacker, quickly growing and dissolving their flesh. These spores deal @Damage[4d6[persistent,poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic] save).

action->Throne Tethered.name: Throne Tethered
action->Throne Tethered.description: Vintalax is attached to his throne by a twisted length of thorny fungal vines that limit his mobility to areas B9 and B10. This tether can be destroyed (AC 30, Hardness 12, HP 50 (BT 25), Immunities acid, bludgeoning; Resistances piercing 10), in which case Vintalax howls in pain as his body rapidly begins to decay. Each round that he remains severed from the throne he loses his fast healing and takes @Damage[4d6[spirit]] damage at the start of his turn, but his AC increases to 36 and his speed to 25 feet, due to his increased mobility. He can end this effect by sitting back down on his throne (an Interact action) and remaining there until the end of his turn, at which point a new fungus tether reattaches to him.

action->Rotting Gift.name: Rotting Gift
action->Rotting Gift.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:34]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 stupefied 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day)

Stage 4 drained 1 and controlled by the Rotting God (1 day)

Stage 5 controlled by the Rotting God (1 day)

Stage 6 the creature becomes permanently controlled by the Rotting God (and may gain unique fungus-themed abilities)—at this point the creature is no longer diseased, and the control can only be ended by the death of the Rotting God or by an effect that removes curses. The creature loses any fungus-themed abilities granted by rotting gift as soon as it's removed.

action->Spore Breath.name: Spore Breath
action->Spore Breath.description: Vintalax unleashes a cloud of spores from his mouth in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30]. Creatures in this area take @Damage[12d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:34|basic|options:area-effect] save). Those who fail this save are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure) as the spores enter their lungs and begin to grow. These creatures take @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage as long as they remain sickened. This bleed damage cannot be stopped as long as the sickened condition persists. Any creature that takes damage from spore breath is exposed to rotting gift.

action->Tentacle Eruption.name: Tentacle Eruption
action->Tentacle Eruption.description: Vintalax drives his tentacles into the ground, causing tendrils of fungus to erupt everywhere in area B9. This deals @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning],3d6[acid]]{2d8 bludgeoning damage plus 3d6 acid} damage to each creature in the area (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save). Creatures that fail their save against tentacle eruption are grasped by the tendrils, reducing their speed by 10 feet until the start of Vintalax's next turn (or are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} on a critical failure).

Vixivax.name: Vixivax
Vixivax.publicNotes:
Ein Invidiak hat keinen Körper und entsteht aus einem tiefen Neidgefühl aufgrund dieses Mangels. Ein Invidiak fühlt sich nur dann richtig „lebendig“, wenn er von einem Wirt Besitz ergriffen hat. In vielen Fällen bleibt ein Invidiak in einem solchen Wirt inaktiv und verbringt Monate oder sogar Jahre damit, von innen heraus zu beobachten und langsam seinen Einfluss auf den Wirt aufzubauen, um dessen Freunden und Familie so viel lang anhaltende Verzweiflung und emotionales Trauma wie möglich zuzufügen.

Langandauernde Besessenheit

Ein Invidiak kann einen bevorzugten Wirt für unbegrenzte Zeit in Besitz nehmen, und im Laufe der Jahre kann sein Einfluss dazu führen, dass der Wirt seltsame neue und einzigartige übernatürliche Kräfte erlangt, die aus dieser spirituellen Verderbnis entstehen. Solche Kräfte funktionieren nur, solange die Besessenheit andauert; wird der Invidiak aus dem Körper vertrieben, verschwinden diese Kräfte und kehren nicht zurück, selbst wenn der Invidiak später denselben Wirt wieder in Besitz nimmt. Diese einzigartigen Invidiaks sind in der Regel viel mächtiger als der hier vorgestellte typische Vertreter.

action->Exorcism Vulnerability.name: Aversion gegen Exorzismus
action->Exorcism Vulnerability.description:
Wenn ein Invidiak versucht, von einer Kreatur @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wU6hNzK8Yfqdmc8m]{Besitz zu ergreifen}, und die Kreatur bei ihrem Rettungswurf zum Widerstehen einen Kritischen Erfolg erzielt oder wenn die Besessenheit einer Kreatur durch einen Invidiak vorzeitig beendet wird, z. B. durch den Zauber @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Magie bannen}, erleidet der Invidiak @Damage[9d6[mental]]{9W6 Mentalen Schaden} und kann 24 Stunden lang keine Besessenheit wirken.

Siehe außerdem Iravalinns Reaktion Kampf um die Kontrolle.

action->Share Pain.name: Schmerz teilen
action->Share Pain.description:
Auslöser Eine Kreatur innerhalb von 30 Fuß/9 m um den Invidiak verursacht dem Invidiak Schaden

Effekt Ein Echo des Schmerzes des Invidiaks greift nach der auslösenden Kreatur und verursacht Nichttödlichen Mentalen Schaden in Höhe der Hälfte des Schadens, den der Invidiak durch den auslösenden Angriff erlitten hat (@Check[will|dc:33|basic]{einfacher Willenswurf}). Bei einem Kritischen Fehlschlag erleidet das Ziel den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Kränkelnd 1}.

action->Favored Host.name: Bevorzugter Wirt
action->Favored Host.description:
Vixivax‘ bevorzugte Art ist Elf.

Jeder Invidiak hat eine bestimmte Art von Lebewesen, von der er bevorzugt Besitz ergreift, ausgewählt aus der folgenden Liste: Aberration, Bestie, eine beliebige Art von Humanoiden (z. B. Elf, Mensch oder Zwerg) oder Tier - in den meisten Fällen ist die bevorzugte Zielart der Mensch. Wenn ein Invidiak Besessenheit gegen einen bevorzugten Wirt einsetzt, erhöht sich die Dauer der Besessenheit auf unbegrenzt, und die Wirkung hält an, bis sie gebannt wird oder der Invidiak sie aufhebt.

action->Shadow Blend.name: Mit Schatten verschmelzen
action->Shadow Blend.description: Der Invidiak nutzt die Aktion Laufen bis zu dem Doppelten seiner Bewegungsrate. Wenn der Invidiak seine Bewegung in einem Bereich mit Dämmerlicht beendet, erhält er den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Versteckt}, solange er sich nicht bewegt und die Beleuchtung in dem Bereich, in dem er sich befindet, nicht heller wird als Dämmerlicht.

action->Unbodied Possession.name: Körperlose Besessenheit
action->Unbodied Possession.description: Vixivax hinterlässt keinen Körper, wenn er @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wU6hNzK8Yfqdmc8m]{Besessenheit} benutzt.

Void Bars.name: Void Bars
Void Bars.description: Bars made of pure Void energy block access to the far side of the cell.
Void Bars.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:45] (master) three times to deface the well-@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} focusing runes etched along the walls, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (10th rank; counteract DC 38) to counteract the void bars
Void Bars.reset: The trap deactivates at the end of a round in which no living creatures are in the cell, then immediately resets at the start of the next round.
Void Bars.routine: (1 action) Each set of void bars manifests a long arm made of darkness that reaches out and attempts to Strike a creature in the room, favoring targets that previously damaged the bars or attempted to disable them, but otherwise choosing a target randomly from those available. This void talon can extend to attack any creature in the room or within 20 feet of the cell door in the outer hall, but targets in the outer hall gain the benefit of cover against this attack.
Void Bars.stealth: (master) to recognize the bars aren't just a passive defense; noticing the bars themselves has a DC of 0

action->Void Pulse.name: Void Pulse
action->Void Pulse.description:
Trigger A living creature comes within five feet of the void bars, or the void bars take damage (the Python does not trigger this hazard, and his presence suppresses this trigger)

Effect Both sets of void bars pulse with dark energy, blasting waves unlife into the area. All living creatures inside the cell or within 20 feet of the cell's entrance in the outer hall must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure; creatures in the outer hall gain a +2 circumstance bonus on this save). If at least one PC becomes drained by a void pulse, psychic alerts ripple through the Oubliette and the party accrues 1 Awareness Point. The void bars then roll initiative.

Witchbole's Heart.name: Witchbole's Heart
Witchbole's Heart.description: A sphere of tangled branches and roots, encrusted with fungus and mold, suddenly animates and reaches out several claw-like limbs.
Witchbole's Heart.disable: @Check[nature|dc:50] to exorcise the corrupted spirit trapped within the Witchbole's Heart, or @Check[thievery|dc:47] three times to deactivate the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} runes scribed in the sphere that allow the corrupted spirit to animate limbs (each one of these successful Thievery checks reduce the hazard's routine by 1 action)
Witchbole's Heart.reset: The Witchbole's Heart deactivates and resets automatically 1 minute after no targets remain.
Witchbole's Heart.routine: (3 actions) The Witchbole's Heart forms several long, thin, spiky limbs tipped with poisonous talons. On each of its actions, it attempts to Strike one non-demon creature in reach of the central sphere. The Witchbole's Heart does can't attempt to Strike a single target more than once per round.
Witchbole's Heart.stealth: (legendary) to notice the sphere is hazardous; noticing the sphere itself has a DC of 0

action->Soulshriek.name: Soulshriek
action->Soulshriek.description:
Trigger a non-demon creature ends their turn in area D3, or a demon in area D3 issues a command to the Witchbole's Heart to awaken

Effect A distorted arboreal's face forms on the side of the Witchbole's Heart and issues a horrific shriek that sounds as if it were issued through an immense throat made of grinding wood. All non-demon creatures in area D3 or within 30 feet of any entrance to area D3 take @Damage[10d6[spirit]] damage (@Check[will|dc:41|basic] save) as their own souls twist and writhe in pain. The trap then rolls initiative.

Withermold.name: Withermold
Withermold.description: Coils of pale yellow thorny fungal vines on which grow hundreds of brown puffballs suddenly whip into frenzied life.
Withermold.disable: @Check[religion|dc:33] (master) twice to exorcise the unholy presence within the withermold, or @Check[survival|dc:35] (master) twice to safely remove the sensory tendrils growing in the mold
Withermold.reset: A withermold deactivates at the end of any round when there are no targets within reach to attack with thorny vine Strikes. It resets immediately unless it's used all its Spore Burst reactions for the day, in which case the withermold resets in 24 hours.
Withermold.routine: (3 actions) A withermold makes three Strikes with its thorny vines on its turn, splitting its Strikes up among potential targets before striking a single target more than once.
Withermold.stealth: (master) (or +30 and expert if a PC critically succeeded at Recalling Knowledge about the campsite's condition)

action->Spore Burst.name: Spore Burst
action->Spore Burst.description:
Frequency three times per day

Trigger A Small or larger creature touches or attempts to enter one of the three tents

Effect The thorny vines of the three withermolds growing on the tents suddenly whip into a frenzy, filling the air with clouds of toxic spores. All creatures within a tent or within 10 feet of a tent's exterior take @Damage[8d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). The withermolds then roll initiative.

Zauglagaul.name: Zauglagaul
Zauglagaul.publicNotes:
Die Magie, die Naturdrachen durchströmt, kann einer bestimmten Art von Drachen animalische oder bestialische Züge verleihen. Dazu gehört vor allem das gewaltige Paar Hörner des Horndrachen. Ihr massiger Körperbau, ihre natürliche Färbung und ihre markanten, gezahnten Schuppen sind zwar alle auf ihre Weise bemerkenswert, es sind jedoch die Hörner, die auf den ersten Blick am auffälligsten sind. Horndrachen benutzen ihre Hörner, um ihre Beute aufzuspießen und so ihre Macht schnell und brutal zu demonstrieren. Sie sind in der Regel nachdenklich und auf Wissen und Selbstdisziplin fixiert - Eigenschaften, die ihr bestialisches Aussehen Lügen strafen. Daher sind Horndrachen im Allgemeinen offener für Gespräche mit Außenstehenden.

Drachen gibt es in unzähligen Formen. Viele von ihnen haben magische Fähigkeiten oder Verbindungen zur Magie. Manche Drachen ziehen mehr Kraft aus der Magie als andere, sodass sie bei längerem Kontakt mit Magie Fähigkeiten entwickeln oder ihren Körper verändern können. Diese Drachen werden immer mächtiger, je älter sie werden und je stärker ihre Verbindung zu ihren magischen Ursprüngen ist. Gelehrte streiten über die Klassifizierung dieser Drachen. Einige bevorzugen den Namen magische Drachen, andere nennen sie aufgrund ihrer Verbindung zu bestimmten magischen Traditionen Traditionsdrachen. Unabhängig von ihren Namen haben diese Drachen eine Reihe von Merkmalen gemeinsam: ihre Fähigkeit, magische Energien anzuzapfen, wachsende Macht und Gerissenheit, sowie sie älter werden, und die Neigung, große Mengen an Schätzen und Reichtum zu horten.
Zauglagaul.saveDetails: +1 Zustandsbonus auf alle RW gg. Natur

action->Camouflage.name: Tarnung
action->Camouflage.description: Zauglagaul kann sich außerhalb von Gebäuden im Dornenwirr @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Verstecken}, auch wenn er keine Deckung hat.

action->Mist Vision.name: Nebelsicht
action->Mist Vision.description: Nebel und Dunst (wie beispielsweise der Schleier) beeinträchtigen Zauglagauls Sicht nicht; er ignoriert den durch Nebel und Dunst verursachten Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen}.

action->Miasma.name: Miasma
action->Miasma.description:
20 Fuß/6 m. Nachdem der Drache Giftodem eingesetzt hat, gibt sein Körper 1 Runde lang eine Wolke aus giftigem Gas ab. Eine Kreatur, die ihren Zug innerhalb des Miasmas beendet, nimmt @Damage[5d6[poison]|options:area-damage]{5W6 Giftschaden} (@Check[fortitude|dc:39|basic|options:area-effect]{einfacher Zähigkeitswurf}).

Kreaturen innerhalb des Miasmas erhalten den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Verborgen} und behandeln alle anderen Kreaturen als Verborgen. Der Drache kann jedoch durch das Miasma hindurchsehen und den Zustand ignorieren.

action->Old Wound.name: Alte Wunde
action->Old Wound.description: Nachdem Zuldanavox vor Jahrzehnten fast Zauglagauls rechten Flügel abgebissen hatte, ist die Wunde nie ganz verheilt. Ein halbmondförmiger Fleck verfärbter Schuppen an der Basis seines rechten Flügels zeugt von dieser Verletzung. Die SC weiß möglicherweise bereits von dieser Wunde (siehe „Zauglagaul im Nachteil“ weiter unten); andernfalls erfährt ein SC nur dann von dieser Schwäche, wenn er Zeuge eines Kritischen Treffers gegen Zauglagaul wird, der Stichschaden verursacht. Sobald ein SC von Zauglagauls alter Wunde weiß, kann er während seines Zuges @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Suchen} nutzen, um sich auf diese alte Wunde zu konzentrieren, wodurch Zauglagaul bis zum Beginn seines nächsten Zuges automatisch als @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß} behandelt wird. Wenn ein SC Zauglagaul in derselben Runde, in der er auf diese Weise Sucht, einen Kritischen Treffer versetzt, erhält der Drache durch den Schmerz für 1 Stunde die Zustände @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Kraftlos 1} und @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Unbeholfen 1}. Mehrere Kritische Treffer können den Wert dieser Zustände auf maximal @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Kraftlos 4} und @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Unbeholfen 4} erhöhen.

action->Twisting Tail.name: Windender Schwanz
action->Twisting Tail.description:
Auslöser Eine Kreatur innerhalb des Angriffsradius des Schwanzangriffs des Drachen benutzt eine Bewegungsaktion oder verlässt ein Feld während einer Bewegungsaktion.

Effekt Der Drache führt einen Schwanzangriff gegen die auslösende Kreatur aus mit einem Malus von -2. Bei einem Treffer wird die Aktion der Kreatur Unterbrochen.

action->Forest Passage.name: Waldmarsch
action->Forest Passage.description: Der Horndrache ignoriert Schwieriges Gelände, welches durch Pflanzen wie Sträucher, Ranken oder Gebüsch bedingt ist. Auch mit Magie manipulierte Pflanzen können den Drachen nicht aufhalten.

action->Impaling Charge.name: Aufspießender Ansturm
action->Impaling Charge.description:
Anforderungen Der Drache hat keine Kreatur auf seinem Horn aufgespießt.

Effekt Der Drache versucht, eine Kreatur aufzuspießen. Er Läuft und führt dann einen Hornangriff aus. Bei einem Treffer wird das Ziel auf dem Horn des Drachen aufgespießt. Die Kreatur erhält, solange sie aufgespießt ist, den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Gegriffen} (und kann auf normale Weise Entkommen). Der Drache muss keine weiteren Aktionen aufwenden, um den Zustand Gegriffen aufrechtzuerhalten. Wenn der Drache sich bewegt, nimmt er die gegriffene Kreatur mit.

action->Spore Breath.name: Sporenodem
action->Spore Breath.description:
Zauglagaul atmet eine giftige Sporenwolke aus, die in einem @Template[cone|distance:60]{60 Fuß/18 m Kegel} @Damage[18d6[poison]|options:area-damage]{18W6 Giftschaden} verursacht. Kreaturen im WirkungsBereich Müssen einen @Check[fortitude|dc:39|options:area-effect,inflicts:stupefied]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen. Zauglagaul kann Sporenodem danach [[/gmr 1d4 #Runden]{1W4 Runden} lang nicht wieder einsetzen.

Kritischer Erfolg Die Kreatur erleidet keinen Schaden.

Erfolg Die Kreatur erleidet nur halben Schaden.

Fehlschlag Die Kreatur erleidet den vollen Schaden und erhält für eine unbegrenzte Dauer den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Benommen}, während die Sporen Wurzeln schlagen und das Opfer unter ablenkenden Halluzinationen leiden lassen.

Kritischer Fehlschlag Die Kreatur erleidet doppelten Schaden und erhält für eine unbegrenzte Dauer den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Benommen 2}

action->Trackless Journey.name: Spurlose Reise
action->Trackless Journey.description: Der Horndrache erhält in natürlicher Umgebung jederzeit die Vorteile von Spuren verwischen, selbst wenn er sich mit voller Bewegungsrate bewegt.

Ziberae.name: Ziberae
Ziberae.publicNotes:
Powerful vrolikais command the untamed armies of demonkind, uniting them behind their one unifying desire for death and destruction. Unlike other demons, the dreaded vrolikai doesn't form directly from a single soul—they instead manifest when a demon devours so many damned souls that their own individual desires are lost in the sinful cacophony. A vrolikai who survives this process gains great power and can claim a region of the Outer Rifts as their own domain.

Vrolikais' enthusiastic embrace of the multitude of sins makes them uniquely suited to lead and unite demons, such that even demon lords often must rely on vrolikais to command their forces. The chaotic and conflicting motivations of demonkind leave little room to find common ground, but the vrolikai can expound upon the beauty of every kind of sin while marching demon armies to battle.

When a sinful mortal soul is judged and sent on to the Outer Rifts, it can become a deadly fiend—a demon. Demons are living incarnations of sin—be they classic sins like wrath or gluttony, or more "specialized" depravities like an obsession with torture or the act of treason or treachery. Once formed, a demon's driving goals are twofold—the amassing of personal power, and the corruption of mortal souls to cause them to become tainted by sin. In this way demons ensure a never-ending supply of new demons to bolster their ever-growing ranks in the Outer Rifts.

Demons are selfish and self-absorbed creatures, and most firmly believe that mortals only play at being more virtuous than fiends. They enjoy tempting mortals into damnation to both indulge their egos and swell their armies. Like many other fiends, one of the great rewards of this manipulation is fulfilling their hunger for souls. In their eyes, the primary use for these souls is to spawn new demons, who can serve as soldiers, slaves, pawns, or even currency for their more powerful masters.

spell->Truesight (Constant).name:

Truesight (Constant)

spell->Translocate (At Will).name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]
Translocate (At Will)

action->Telepathy 100 Feet.name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.kdhbPaBMK1d1fpbA]
Telepathy 100 Feet

action->Death-Stealing Gaze.name: Death-Stealing Gaze
action->Death-Stealing Gaze.description:
30 feet. When a non-demon ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:38] save. If it fails, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

A creature that dies while it has drain from a vrolikai's gaze rises as a ghoul the next midnight. The GM determines what kind of ghoul.

action->Black Spore Knives.name: Black Spore Knives
action->Black Spore Knives.description: Ziberae manifests a sickle-shaped blade of what looks like seething black fungus in four of her hands (or three of them if she's wielding his apotheosis knife in one hand). These weapons function as +2 greater striking daggers that deal an additional 2d6 poison damage. On a critical hit, the additional poison damage inflicted by a black spore knife is persistent poison damage. They fade away into nothingness 1 minute after Ziberae no longer carries them.

action->Consume Death.name: Consume Death
action->Consume Death.description:
The vrolikai focuses their deathstealing gaze upon a single target they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against death-stealing gaze.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is affected by death-stealing gaze and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}. If the creature was already drained 1 by the death-stealing gaze before attempting the save, a failed save increases the value of the drained condition by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4}. The vrolikai gains 10 temporary Hit Points, and the drained creature is temporarily immune until the start of the vrolikai's next turn.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature increases the amount of drain by 2.

action->Focused Apotheosis.name: Focused Apotheosis
action->Focused Apotheosis.description:
Zibrerae attacks a single target with all of their black spore knives. The demon makes a black spore knife Strike with the following additional effects. This counts toward Zibrerae's multiple attack penalty as a number of attacks equal to the number of back spore knives Zibrerae used.

Ziberae can use her apotheosis knife in place of one of her black spore knives. When she does so, she attacks with her apotheosis knife Strike instead of a black spore knife Strike.

Critical Success The target takes an additional 2d6 void damage for each knife beyond the first (typically @Damage[6d6[void]]{6d6 extra damage}) and takes @Damage[4d6[persistent,void]] damage.

Success The target takes an additional 2d6 void damage for each knife beyond the first.

Failure The vrolikai deals the damage their black flame knife Strike normally deals on a hit.

action->Mindwarping.name: Mindwarping
action->Mindwarping.description:
The sting of a vrolikai is mind-warping. A creature struck must attempt a @Check[will|dc:44] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Failure The creature becomes stupefied 1. If it's already stupefied, its stupefied value increases by 1 instead (to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}).

Critical Failure As failure, plus the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute.
